metrics.narrative: No quantified business impact exists anywhere: Customer_Business_Problem__c, Value_to_Customer__c and Compelling_Event__c are all blank. The only substantive signal is a generic interest note, 'interested in demo and POC of spend analysis' (Next_Step_History__c, Karson Keogh, 31 Mar 2026, ~14 weeks ago), with no dollar figure, process metric, or stated urgency attached to it.

champion.narrative: X10b_Champion_Business_Buyer__c is blank and no named individual exists at Grupo Argos or within EY. EY itself is the closest thing to an internal advocate, and its behavior has degraded from actively sharing commercials and a business case outline (20 Apr - 26 May 2026) to reporting no update (16 Jun 2026, ~23 days ago) to citing external blockers (leadership change and election slowdown, 29 Jun 2026, ~10 days ago) — EY no longer functions as a reliable advocate, let alone a champion.

competition.narrative: Competitors__c is blank and no rival vendor has ever been named in any source. The real competitive dynamic is inertia: with no Economic Buyer engaged and Grupo Argos reportedly undergoing a leadership change amid an election-driven slowdown (Next_Step__c, 29 Jun 2026), 'Do Nothing' — the initiative quietly stalling under new leadership — is the most concrete threat on record.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain_points_in_the_current_solution__c and Gaps_identified_during_sales_demo__c are both blank, and no Avoma call exists to corroborate a stated problem. The closest thing to pain is Argos's expressed interest in a spend-analysis demo/POC (Next_Step_History__c, 31 Mar 2026), which implies a spend-visibility gap, but the buyer has never articulated the underlying business problem, its cost, or why it matters now.

paper_process.narrative: Current_Contract_Expiration__c is blank and no MSA/SOW/contracting activity exists, which is expected this early (Formal Evaluation, pre-selection) — this gap is not itself a red flag at this stage, but there is also no dated milestone (RFP submission date, decision date) that would let Zycus plan a realistic paper-process timeline once commercial terms are agreed.

economic_buyer.narrative: Executive_Sponsor_Identified__c and Decision_Maker_Identified__c are both false, and there are zero OpportunityContactRole records for Grupo Argos on this opportunity. Two direct Zycus-Argos calls did occur early (27 Jan 2026 and 4 Feb 2026, ~5.5 months ago), but no attendee name or title from either call was ever captured, and there has been no direct Argos contact of any kind since.

decision_process.narrative: What_is_the_decision_process__c and the approval fields are all blank. Structurally, EY runs the entire process on Zycus's behalf: EY requested implementation and SaaS pricing data from Argos (18 Feb 2026), later relayed that Argos wanted POC detail (3 Mar 2026), and most recently reported a meeting with Argos whose outcome was never confirmed back (KK followed up 8 Jun 2026 asking 'how the meeting went'). No internal Argos approval sequence has ever been described to Zycus.

decision_criteria.narrative: Business_Requirements__c is blank and Shoe_Fit_Criteria_Met__c is false. The only proxy is Argos asking, via EY, for 'more information on what a POC would look like' (Next_Step_History__c, 3 Mar 2026, ~18 weeks ago) — a hint that a working proof point matters to their evaluation, but no formal criteria, weighting, or requirements document has ever been shared back with Zycus.

items.name: Amount increased $100K → $350K
items.summary: Deal value jumped 3.5x in one update, signaling a major scope expansion or corrected budget figure rather than incremental upsell — but no note or activity explains the driver, so the reason behind the revision needs to be confirmed.

day_summary.overall: The Grupo Argos deal took a sharp turn upward, with the deal size more than tripling from $100K to $350K in a single update. That kind of jump usually means either a discovery call uncovered a much bigger use case, more business units got pulled into scope, or the buyer clarified their real budget — but there's no accompanying note explaining which. Worth confirming with the deal owner what drove the revision before it's reflected in forecast conversations.

flags: PARTNER-ONLY RELATIONSHIP IS A CRITICAL VULNERABILITY: This deal has zero direct Grupo Argos contacts mapped and zero buyer calls. The sole relationship is through EY, who reported no update on 16 Jun (10 days ago). Partner-intermediated deals with no direct buyer champion or EB access are extremely fragile — we have no control, no intel, and no protection if the partner deprioritizes or the buyer stalls. The immediate move is to demand direct introductions to the Argos evaluation team and Economic Buyer, and to run a joint discovery call with EY + Argos where we can validate the opportunity, map stakeholders, and establish our own relationship. If EY cannot or will not facilitate direct access within 7 business days, escalate to the deal owner's manager to engage EY leadership or disqualify the opportunity.

162 DAYS IN FORMAL EVALUATION WITH the relevant stakeholder: The deal has been in Formal Evaluation since creation (15 Jan, 162 days ago) but there is no evidence of a formal evaluation happening — no demos delivered, no POC executed, no RFI/RFP response, no buyer validation sessions. The Next Step history shows Argos expressed interest in a spend analysis demo and POC on 31 Mar (87 days ago) but neither was delivered. A deal that sits in Formal Evaluation for 5+ months with no buyer-facing milestones is stalled or dead. The re-engagement move must include a concrete near-term validation event (a discovery call, a demo, or a scoping workshop) scheduled within 14 days to prove the deal is still live.

NO OR MAPPED: With zero buyer contact roles and zero calls, we have no visibility into who at Grupo Argos controls the $350k budget, who signs off, or who drives the decision. Commercials with four pricing scenarios were submitted to EY on 20 Apr (67 days ago) but we do not know if they reached the EB or what the reaction was. Mapping the EB and running a direct commercial conversation with them is a prerequisite to any realistic forecast or close plan. This must be a top-3 action in the next 14 days.

Leadership change (29 Jun 2026) may have reset the deal: prior stakeholders may be out of role, budget may be under review, and initiative may be deprioritized under new leadership. Go direct to new Argos CPO, CFO, or transformation lead to revalidate the S2P business case, confirm budget, and anchor the deal to a senior sponsor who will drive it through organizational disruption.
